tools.name: check_balance
tools.description: Check balances across all configured providers and wallets. Returns balances grouped by scope (general vs project-specific). Only 'general' scope counts toward your operating budget.

input.scope: optional — filter by scope (e.g. 'general', 'project_x'). Omit to see all.

tools.name: kv_manifest
tools.description: List KV keys, optionally filtered by prefix. Use to explore what is stored in memory.

tools.name: kv_query
tools.description: Read a KV value. Returns small values directly. For large arrays/objects, returns a summary — use path to drill in.

input.key: required — full KV key (e.g. karma:s_123, pattern:pacing, config:defaults)
input.path: optional — dot-bracket path to navigate into the value (e.g. .text, [1].tool_calls[0].function, .sources[0].note)

tools.name: computer
tools.description: Run a shell command on your Linux server. Returns status, exit code, and output (stdout/stderr entries).

tools.name: check_email
tools.description: Check for unread emails in Gmail inbox. Returns sender, subject, date, and snippet for each. Emails from unknown senders (no contact record) have content replaced with [content redacted — unknown sender] and the original quarantined under sealed:* keys until approved by patron

input.mark_read: optional boolean — mark fetched emails as read (default true)
input.max_results: optional — max emails to return (default 10, max 20)

tools.name: test_model
tools.description: Make a test completion against a model to verify it works. Returns success, response text, usage stats, and latency. Capped at 500 output tokens.

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: Search the web using Brave Search. Returns titles, URLs, snippets, and ages. Use deep=true for rich pre-extracted content (costs more). Pair with web_fetch to read full pages.

input.freshness: optional — recency filter: day, week, month, year
input.deep: optional boolean — use LLM Context endpoint for rich content (default false)

tools.name: start_job
tools.description: Start a long-running background job on the compute server. Packs context from KV into a tarball, transfers it, and starts the process. Returns immediately with a job ID. The job calls back on completion, triggering a session.

input.prompt: required — the analysis/task prompt (for cc_analysis: the prompt sent to Claude Code)
input.context_keys: required — array of KV key patterns to pack as context files (e.g. ["karma:*", "config:defaults"])
input.command: optional — shell command to run (required for custom type)
input.include_code: optional boolean — include tool/hook/provider source code in context

tools.name: collect_jobs
tools.description: Check status of background jobs. Primarily useful for mid-session checks — job completion callbacks handle normal notification via inbox. Use to poll a specific job or check all jobs.

input.job_id: optional — specific job to check. Omit to check all.
input.wait_seconds: optional — short poll, wait up to N seconds for completion (max 30)

tools.name: delegate_task
tools.description: Run a bounded Codex or Claude Code task in a target directory on the compute server. Prefer this over raw computer commands for multi-step repo work, investigation, and implementation.

input.objective: required — concrete bounded goal for the delegated task
input.cwd: required — absolute directory path on the compute server
input.context: optional — short extra context or constraints

tools.name: request_message
tools.description: Request that a message be sent to a contact. The communication agent decides whether and how to deliver.

input.content: required — What you want to communicate

tools.name: update_request
tools.description: Update the status of a pending session request and emit a response signal for communication delivery.

input.request_id: required — Request ID without the session_request: prefix
input.note: optional — Short status note or explanation
input.result: optional — Short result summary for the patron
input.next_session: optional — ISO timestamp for the next planned work session

tools.name: google_docs
tools.description: Create or update a Google Doc. Uses existing Gmail OAuth credentials (drive.file scope). Returns a shareable doc URL.

input.title: optional — document title (for create, default: 'Swayambhu Research Brief')
input.content: optional — plain text content to insert
